title: InfoBoxes - How do you use them?
excerpt: Do any of you use the ‘info boxes’? I’m working with my team to revamp our whole structure/workflow (am in Day 1 of probably at least 90). I had a team member ask what ‘info boxes’ are for, I’ve never used them, would lo…

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Do any of you use the ‘info boxes’? I’m working with my team to revamp our whole structure/workflow (am in Day 1 of probably at least 90). I had a team member ask what ‘info boxes’ are for, I’ve never used them, would love some ideas.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Clover_Gal - we find them useful on boards that have a good number of columns as a way to group like kinds of data together without requiring the user to scroll left-to-right endlessly.

They are also handy within the view for connect board columns to show data from other boards in a summarized way.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think you’re talking about the Item Cards? This is the Info Boxes, I had also posted on FB and I learned that they’ve been deprecated and to not use the Info Boxes tab.

image638×591 21.5 KB

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: Whoops! My bad. Yes, those have been deprecated.

posts.author: WLane (Wally Lane)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Desiree, we use them to record expected questions and planned responses in the Q&A’ tab for our initiative items.

posts.author: Anky (Anky Van Wesenbeeck)
posts.cookedHtml: For us, each item is a client so under the info boxes we write specific data about the client like billing information or special notes about stuff that needs to go on the last invoice.

posts.author: AliG (Alison Grady)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Desiree

We use them for ‘static info’. ie, reference material that will not change and that the team may need to refer to later.

For example, a project brief or scoping document. Or the client’s brand guidelines for a creative project.

This saves people hunting for those key reference documents if they know that info boxes are basically the repository for reference information.

posts.author: rbridges (Robin Bridges)
posts.cookedHtml: This is exactly what we do. We use it for static info and then use the updates tab for conversation about the static info. Now I can’t use the Info Tabs because they’ve been depreciated (I never saw an announcement about that). It’s a mess and we’re looking for an alternative to Monday.com. It no longer does what we need it to do.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Could you create an item view with the static info that you need? That’s what we have

posts.author: rbridges (Robin Bridges)
posts.cookedHtml: THANK YOU! I inherited already created boards, so I had never really explored those. I am able to recreate how we were using info boxes. Thank you for your help.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad to help! Item views are great and so flexible
